2606.31819.title: Creating Intelligence: A Computational Foundation for AGI
2606.31819.paper_summary: This work introduces a new computational theory of mind grounded in set theory and hyperdimensional computing. Whereas traditional neural networks rely on continuous weights and matrix multiplication, this framework works with sparse binary data. It represents information as discrete sets, directly modeling biological neural population codes. I demonstrate that associative memory emerges naturally from network topologies featuring a combinatorially expanded hidden layer. Learning is driven by topological plasticity rather than scalar weight adjustments. This architecture unifies auto-associative and hetero-associative learning under a single core algorithm: information retrieval via subset pattern matching and exact nearest-neighbor search. Operating with constant-time complexity, these mechanisms bridge perceptual data (sparse distributed representations) and symbols (sparse holographic representations) without continuous bottlenecks. Mapping this framework to neuroanatomy, I propose that both the cerebellum and the neocortex implement variants of this algorithm, making subset pattern matching the fundamental engine of cognition. Because it relies on discrete logic rather than matrix arithmetic, this algorithm translates directly into in-memory hardware. This opens a new route toward synthetic intelligence with human-level energy efficiency.
2606.31819.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作介紹了一種基於集合論和超維計算的新計算心智理論。<lb/>傳統的神經網絡依賴於連續權重和矩陣乘法，而這個框架則使用稀疏的二進制數據。<lb/>它將信息表示為離散集合，直接建模生物神經群體編碼。<lb/>我展示了聯想記憶如何自然地從具有組合擴展隱藏層的網絡拓撲中產生。<lb/>學習是由拓撲可塑性驅動，而不是標量權重調整。<lb/>這種架構將自聯想學習和異聯想學習統一在一個核心算法之下：通過子集模式匹配和精確的最近鄰搜索來檢索信息。<lb/>這些機制以常數時間複雜度運行，橋接感知數據（稀疏分佈表示）和符號（稀疏全息表示），而不會出現連續瓶頸。<lb/>將這個框架映射到神經解剖學，我提出小腦和新皮層都實現了這個算法的變體，使子集模式匹配成為認知的基本引擎。<lb/>因為它依賴於離散邏輯而不是矩陣算術，這個算法可以直接轉換為內存硬體。<lb/>這為實現具有人類水平能效的合成智能開辟了一條新路徑。
2606.31819.author: Peter Overmann et.al.
